experiment: ML Standalone — retrain deployment model on 500-symbol universe
hypothesis: Current deployment model trained on only 152 symbols (Phase 31). The expanded 500-symbol cache (Phase 35) should let the model scan 3.3x more names, yielding more high-conviction trades at the same per-trade quality.
verdict: DEPLOYED — 500-symbol model is better in aggregate OOS; deployed to live paper trader
method: Trained XGBoost on the narrow-SL (0.5%) 500-symbol dataset (4,003,893 bars). Compared via (a) full-universe 4-fold walk-forward (fixed thr 0.80) and (b) 5-symbol live-path backtest (WIPRO,TATAELXSI,DIXON,RELIANCE,ONGC, 730d).
analysis: 5-symbol live-path test is ~equal (+28.3k vs +31.8k) because per-trade quality is similar (~630 vs ~740/trade). The real gain is UNIVERSE BREADTH: the 500-symbol model scans 3.3x more names, so the full-universe walk-forward shows +223,727 vs +49,985 (4.5x more total net) at the same per-trade quality. The improvement is in trade COUNT, not per-trade edge.

deployment.action: Replaced data/ml_strategy_model.json + _meta.json with the 500-symbol versions (features identical: 35, sl 0.005, LONG+SHORT). Threshold 0.80 (validated in walk-forward).
deployment.paper_trader: Restarted PID 78376 (was 77901). Preserved state (no --reset). Paper mode, no --real. Healthy: 168 symbols, 5 strategies, 0 errors.
deployment.note: Old deployment used thr 0.75 (Phase E); new model thr 0.80 (more selective, validated). Live selectivity slightly tighter — consistent with walk-forward test.

files.trained_model: data/ml_strategy_model_500.json + _meta (kept as the source of the deployment copy)
